description_long: This is a medium-sized acorn squash pumpkin with a deeply ribbed, lobed surface and a mottled coloration of dark green and orange-yellow patches. The skin appears tough and slightly matte, with irregular speckles and streaks of orange distributed unevenly across the surface. The pumpkin has a visible, short, woody stem at one end, and the opposite end is slightly pointed. Its overall shape is somewhat asymmetrical, with pronounced ridges and valleys running from the stem to the base. The surface texture looks firm and hard, with a natural, rustic appearance typical of garden-grown squash. There are no visible carvings, facial features, or decorative accessories present.
description: A mottled green and orange acorn squash pumpkin with deep ridges, a tough skin, and a short stem.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the stem and the radiating deep ridges with mottled green and orange coloration.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the pronounced lobes and the stem protruding from the left side.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view displaying the pointed base and the elongated ridges with more visible orange streaks.
description_view_3: Angled side view emphasizing the asymmetrical shape and the mix of green and orange patches.
